commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Patch repair is not needed; the remaining action is maintainer message-delivery review and duplicate-candidate selection.; Cleared: The diff only changes message-tool routing logic and focused tests; it does not touch dependencies, workflows, credentials, permissions, lockfiles, or artifact execution paths. commands.review_summary:
The PR defaults internal/WebChat-created message tools to `message_tool_only` when no mode is supplied, feeds that mode into the tool description and outbound runner, and adds focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +32. Total +38 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main leaves WebChat-created message tools without `message_tool_only`, while the internal sink requires that mode and the fallback normalization path throws the reported target error.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Patch repair is not needed; the remaining action is maintainer message-delivery review and duplicate-candidate selection.

target.title: fix #96840: [Bug]: Targetless message.send fails with 'Action send requires a target' in WebChat despite docs stating source-reply sink should handle it
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

Root cause: active WebChat message tools were constructed with the internal `webchat` current-channel context, but they did not set `sourceReplyDeliveryMode` to `message_tool_only`. A targetless `message.send` therefore skipped the internal source-reply sink, reached normal outbound target validation without an inferred target, and failed with `Action send requires a target` instead of projecting into the current WebChat run.

Why does this matter now?

Users in an active WebChat conversation can reasonably use `tools.message` for visible progress updates or intermediate results. The documentation says those targetless sends should project into the same active chat, so the current failure blocks a documented user-facing WebChat path.

What is the intended outcome?

Root cause fix: when the message tool is built for the internal WebChat current source, default its source-reply delivery mode to `message_tool_only`, while preserving an explicit caller override such as `sourceReplyDeliveryMode: "automatic"`. This routes targetless WebChat message-tool sends to the existing source-reply sink instead of masking the later target-validation error.

What is intentionally out of scope?

No WebChat reusable outbound channel is added, no Gateway room-event final-reply delivery default is changed, no schema/config/migration behavior is changed, and no external/routed message delivery behavior is changed. Related #96850 covers the same user symptom; this PR is scoped to the message tool construction boundary so ordinary WebChat final replies can remain automatic.

What does success look like?

A targetless `message.send` created inside an active WebChat source context delivers to the current run as an internal UI source reply, and the resulting message is visible through the same Control UI/WebChat `chat.history` transcript projection. Explicit automatic delivery remains honored and existing Gateway source-reply delivery contracts keep passing.

What should reviewers focus on?

The source-of-truth boundary is message tool construction for the internal WebChat current source. The outbound runner and internal source-reply sink already own targetless current-run delivery once `sourceReplyDeliveryMode` is `message_tool_only`; this PR only supplies that missing mode at the canonical tool boundary instead of adding downstream WebChat special cases.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #96840

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #96850

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No maintainer request beyond the linked issue report and documented WebChat behavior.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Targetless `message.send` inside an active WebChat run should deliver to the current WebChat source-reply sink and appear in the same Control UI/WebChat chat history instead of requiring an explicit target.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw worktree with the real `createMessageTool`/outbound message-action path, plus a connected Gateway Control UI WebSocket using the real `chat.history` RPC and session transcript projection path.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts --reporter dot --maxWorkers 1`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts -t "chat.history mirrors current-session message tool sends before NO_REPLY" --reporter dot --maxWorkers 1`
  - Evidence-only local proof: ran a temporary `src/gateway/control-ui-chat-history-visible-proof.test.ts` against the connected Control UI WebSocket harness, then removed the temporary file before submit. It seeded a transcript with a targetless `tools.message` send, called `chat.history`, and printed the returned visible history payload.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

  ```text
  stdout | ../../src/gateway/control-ui-chat-history-visible-proof.test.ts > targetless WebChat message.send appears in Control UI chat.history
  {
    "proof": "targetless WebChat message.send visible in Control UI chat.history",
    "gatewayRpc": "chat.history",
    "sessionKey": "main",
    "targetlessToolCall": {
      "name": "message",
      "arguments": {
        "action": "send",
        "message": "Here, love. Eva, not Evo."
      }
    },
    "rawFinalAssistantReply": "NO_REPLY",
    "visibleTexts": [
      "Evo, you there?",
      "Here, love. Eva, not Evo."
    ],
    "assistantMirror": {
      "role": "assistant",
      "content": [
        {
          "type": "text",
          "text": "Here, love. Eva, not Evo."
        }
      ],
      "openclawMessageToolMirror": {
        "toolName": "message",
        "toolCallId": "call-message-1"
      },
      "timestamp": 2,
      "__openclaw": {
        "seq": 3
      }
    }
  }
  ```

- Observed result after fix: The targetless message-tool call appears in the same `chat.history` result as an assistant message mirror, while the final assistant `NO_REPLY` control reply remains hidden from visible chat history.
- What was not tested: A browser-hosted production WebChat session and an external hosted WebChat deployment were not used.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The visible-delivery proof is local near-real Gateway/Control UI WebSocket proof. It uses the actual `chat.history` RPC and transcript projection path, with a seeded local transcript rather than a hosted browser UI recording.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): The new regression failed before the fix because the captured send input had `sourceReplyDeliveryMode` as `undefined` instead of `message_tool_only`:

  ```text
  FAIL  |agents-core| ../../src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts > message tool secret scoping > defaults internal WebChat message tool sends to the source-reply sink
  AssertionError: expected undefined to be 'message_tool_only'
  ```

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts --reporter dot --maxWorkers 1`

  ```text
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
       Tests  216 passed (216)
  ```

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/tool-resolution.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.send-validation.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/source-reply-delivery-mode.test.ts --reporter dot --maxWorkers 2`

  ```text
  Test Files  5 passed (5)
       Tests  70 passed (70)
  ```

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts -t "chat.history mirrors current-session message tool sends before NO_REPLY" --reporter dot --maxWorkers 1`

  ```text
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  1 passed | 35 skipped (36)
  ```

- Evidence-only visible payload proof with connected Control UI WebSocket `chat.history` harness:

  ```text
  Test Files  3 passed (3)
       Tests  3 passed (3)
  ```

- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- Local commit review result: `nothing_found`, `check_conclusion: success`.

What regression coverage was added or updated?

A focused regression test now locks the WebChat current-source scenario: `createMessageTool` with `currentChannelProvider: "webchat"` and a WebChat session key sends a targetless message with `sourceReplyDeliveryMode: "message_tool_only"`. A second test locks the explicit override scenario so `sourceReplyDeliveryMode: "automatic"` remains unchanged. Existing Gateway chat-history coverage also confirms a successful targetless message-tool send is mirrored into visible Control UI/WebChat history before a silent `NO_REPLY` completion.

What failed before this fix, if known?

The new WebChat regression failed before the fix because the message tool passed `undefined` for `sourceReplyDeliveryMode`, proving the internal source-reply sink could not be selected for the targetless send.

If no test was added, why not?

A focused regression test was added, so no no-test exception is needed. The smallest reliable guardrail is at message tool construction because that is where the WebChat current-source context is known and before outbound target validation can throw the user-visible error. The visible-delivery proof uses existing Gateway/Control UI history projection rather than adding another permanent test file for duplicate coverage.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes ŌĆö active WebChat targetless message-tool sends now deliver to the current run instead of failing target validation.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No. The change does not add config, environment variables, migrations, schemas, or a reusable WebChat outbound channel.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No. The change only selects the existing internal source-reply sink for the already-current WebChat source context; it does not broaden secret access, network routing, auth, or tool execution permissions.

What is the highest-risk area?

Message-delivery behavior for WebChat source replies, especially preserving the existing distinction between automatic final replies and message-tool-only source replies.

How is that risk mitigated?

The patch keeps the change in the message tool construction owner path, preserves explicit `automatic` overrides, and leaves Gateway WebChat room-event final-reply resolution unchanged. Contract tests for Gateway tool resolution, outbound send validation, source-reply delivery mode, and Control UI `chat.history` projection pass, which guards against turning WebChat into a general reusable outbound channel or losing the visible transcript mirror.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Ready for ClawSweeper re-review and maintainer review; maintainer-ready confidence is High.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Author action addressed RF-001/RF-002/RF-003 by adding local connected Control UI WebSocket `chat.history` proof showing the targetless message-tool send appears in the same visible history. CI and maintainer review are pending after this PR body update. RF-004/RF-005 remain maintainer advisory items: there is an older duplicate candidate PR (#96850), so maintainers should choose one landing candidate and close the duplicate only after the chosen fix merges.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- RF-001 fixed with current-head proof: the visible proof shows a targetless `message.send` appears in the same Control UI/WebChat `chat.history` transcript as an assistant mirror.
- RF-002 fixed with current-head proof: the PR body now includes terminal/live output from the connected Control UI WebSocket `chat.history` path, not only source-path JSON.
- RF-003 fixed with current-head proof: the visible transcript payload includes both `visibleTexts` and `openclawMessageToolMirror` for the targetless send.
- RF-004 disclosed as maintainer advisory: #96850 is an older duplicate candidate for the same linked bug, and maintainers should choose the landing branch.
- RF-005 disclosed as maintainer advisory: the remaining non-author action is maintainer message-delivery review and duplicate-candidate selection.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt
